_comment: Canonical Guard System data — single source of truth. NT8 add-on (GA, v1.5.x): 6 trigger conditions x 5 response levels (Notify/Risk Alert/Acknowledge/Trading Pause/Disconnect) per MERIDIAN.md §6. Standalone apps (early access, 0.2.x): the next-generation 6-TIER ladder under 'standalone' below — verified against the app source 2026-06-12. Edit here; pages import this file. Do NOT hardcode triggers/levels in pages.

triggers.name: PSI Below
triggers.desc: Psychological stability drops under a threshold you define.

triggers.name: Consecutive Losses
triggers.desc: N losses in a row within the same session.

triggers.name: Session P&L Below
triggers.desc: Daily realized P&L falls past your declared limit.

triggers.name: Unrealized P&L Below
triggers.desc: Open position floating loss exceeds your threshold — before you close.

triggers.name: Single Trade Loss
triggers.desc: One trade loses more than a defined amount.

triggers.name: Session Time Over
triggers.desc: You have traded longer than you planned for the day.

levels.name: Notify
levels.desc: A quiet toast notification. Stays out of the way of execution.

levels.name: Risk Alert
levels.desc: Persistent HUD banner. Every entry requires active confirmation.

levels.name: Acknowledge
levels.desc: Type your own pre-written phrase and wait out a countdown timer before continuing.

levels.name: Trading Pause
levels.desc: Blocks new entries entirely by cancelling orders. Survives NT8 restart.

levels.name: Disconnect
levels.desc: Severs broker connection. Optional re-disconnect if you try to bypass.

utility.name: Min loss filter
utility.desc: Ignore fee-sized scratches in consecutive-loss streaks.

utility.name: Auto-flatten
utility.desc: Optionally close all open positions when a pause activates.

utility.name: Pause persistence
utility.desc: Lockout survives NinjaTrader restart or crash.

utility.name: Emergency Reset
utility.desc: One click clears all active Guard state if you need an out.

standalone._comment: Guard v2 — first on the standalone Tradovate/Ironbeam apps. Six tiers, each doing exactly one job. Copy mirrors the in-app rule editor verbatim-level facts.

tiers.name: Notify
tiers.short: Just shows a message. Doesn't stop you.
tiers.desc: A brief banner in the HUD corner. Informational only — it never touches execution.

tiers.name: Alert
tiers.short: Shows a message and reads it aloud.
tiers.desc: A persistent HUD warning banner that also reads the message aloud, until the condition clears. Non-blocking.

tiers.name: Acknowledge
tiers.short: Makes you stop and type a phrase.
tiers.desc: A blocking dialog you must dismiss — optionally by typing your own pre-written phrase and waiting out a countdown.

tiers.name: Pause
tiers.short: Stops new trades. You can still exit.
tiers.desc: Cancels new entry orders for the window and re-checks broker truth so no stray order re-opens a position. You stay connected and can always close or reduce — exits and stops are never touched.

tiers.name: Cool-down Wall
tiers.short: Covers your screen so you step away.
tiers.desc: A full-screen cool-down takeover with guided breathing and a countdown. If you hold a position it first offers flatten/manage — it never traps an open trade. You choose a soft wall (can Alt-Tab) or a hard wall (re-asserts focus).

tiers.name: Cut
tiers.short: Blocks orders from leaving your PC.
tiers.desc: Cuts this PC's network path to the broker's order servers via the Windows firewall — a blunt, machine-wide block, so an order physically cannot leave. The one stop that holds even a market order on a prop account. Needs admin rights; lifts automatically when the window ends.

perRuleOptions.name: Reverse market order
perRuleOptions.desc: If a market order slips through in the firing instant, immediately send the opposite order (costs a round-trip commission).

perRuleOptions.name: Soft / hard wall
perRuleOptions.desc: Soft can be Alt-Tabbed for emergencies; hard re-asserts focus until the timer releases you.

perRuleOptions.name: Flatten-first / flat-only
perRuleOptions.desc: Either flatten positions first and then cut, or only cut while you are already flat.

perRuleOptions.name: Broker preset
perRuleOptions.desc: Which broker's order endpoints to block — defaults to the connected broker.

safety.name: Fail-open by design
safety.desc: Anything that lifts a Guard state runs on the clock, never gated behind a live data feed — a Cut or a dropped stream can never lock the machine permanently. A watchdog and Force-reset both release the firewall.

safety.name: Honest enforcement
safety.desc: If Guard cannot enforce (for example an expired session token), it says so on screen instead of pretending the lock is in place.

safety.name: Broker-level lock
safety.desc: On Tradovate demo and eval accounts, Guard can additionally write the platform's own risk lock (liquidate-only) — enforcement at the broker, not just in the app. Funded/live accounts do not expose this API; Ironbeam enforcement is app-level.
